description_long: This object is a full-height turnstile, typically used for access control in secure urban environments such as subway stations, stadiums, or building entrances. It features two tall, parallel vertical columns connected by a horizontal top enclosure, which houses an integrated access control panel with a keypad and display. The structure is robust and metallic, designed to prevent unauthorized passage while allowing controlled entry. The passageway is defined by the space between the columns, with the rotating arms (not visible in these views) likely positioned within the frame. The exterior is finished in a matte gray, giving it a utilitarian and industrial appearance. The overall design emphasizes durability, security, and ease of integration with electronic access systems.
description: A tall, metallic full-height turnstile with a rectangular frame and integrated access control panel, designed for secure entry in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the access control panel and entry slot on the top enclosure between the two vertical columns.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid, rectangular profile of the turnstile with no visible access features.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front but without the access control panel, showing the plain back of the top enclosure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with a solid, featureless vertical surface.
